facts.claim: Chess looks like a board game, but it's an infinity simulator.
Chess teaches humans how to confront the horrible infinite.
Chess began about 1500 years ago in India with a game called Chataranga.
Chataranga evolved into a similar game called Shatrange in Persia a century or so later.
The Persians introduced the idea of checkmates, derived from 'Shak' (king) and 'Shakmat' (the king is boned), which ends the game.
Europe introduced castling and two-square advancing pawns to chess.
Originally, the queen piece was called the adviser and could only move one diagonal square.
In the 15th century, the queen's movement was expanded to its modern powerful form, possibly in honor of powerful ladies like Queen Isabella of Spain.
Originally in chess, the bishop pieces were elephants.
Chess is really useful for metaphors.
Zwang is a chess concept where a player finds themselves in a position where every move is a bad one, but they have to move.
Chess is a collaboration, but ultimately players are completely alone in their mistakes, similar to life.
Chess is all about learning to be defeated with dignity.
Great chess players are artists, not computers.
Great chess players dance with infinity.
Mikall Tal's strategy was to take opponents into a deep dark forest where two and two make five and the path leading out is only wide enough for one.
The chess opening D4, D5, then C4 is known as the Queen's Gambit declined.
The early game Chataranga had no queen but included elephants, and a four-player version existed.
Emperor Teima in Central Asia introduced variations to chess that included giraffes, camels, and war engines.
The Chinese added cannons to chess variations.
By the 1800s, chess rules were largely the same as the modern rules.
The king piece can move one square in any direction.
The knight pieces have always moved in an L-shape.
Europeans dropped the elephant piece (which became the bishop) from chess because they didn't use war elephants.
Rooks, also known as castles, were originally called chariots.
Rooks can only move vertically and horizontally.
Fisher random is a chess variation where the back rank pieces are scrambled.
Anti-chess is a variation where the goal is to lose as hard as possible.
Islamic philosophers believed chess taught patience and reasoning.
For Europeans, chess was seen as a miniaturized battleground.
